description:
You are working as a web-developer who must quickly back up the current working copy of a site.

Pre-existing material:
• Your project lives in the directory  
  /home/user/web_project  
  It already contains these items (exact spellings and case):
    /home/user/web_project/index.html  
    /home/user/web_project/css/style.css  
    /home/user/web_project/js/app.js  
    /home/user/web_project/img/logo.png
• An empty directory for backups is ready at  
  /home/user/backups

Objective (single-command difficulty):
1. Create a gzip-compressed tar archive of the entire /home/user/web_project directory.  
2. The archive file must be named exactly  
   /home/user/backups/site_backup.tar.gz

Validation specifics the grader will enforce:
A. The file /home/user/backups/site_backup.tar.gz must exist.  
B. When the grader extracts that archive into an empty temporary folder, it must recreate the *relative* directory structure shown above. In other words, after extraction the grader must find:  
   ./web_project/index.html  
   ./web_project/css/style.css  
   ./web_project/js/app.js  
   ./web_project/img/logo.png  
   (No extra or missing files are allowed.)
C. File contents and timestamps inside the archive are not inspected; only presence and correct paths matter.

No other output, log files, or on-screen messages are required—only the correctly named archive in the specified location.

You should accomplish everything with a single terminal command (e.g., one tar invocation with appropriate flags).
name: task_000000_bbf67bce
